visible_structure: SKILL.md with name and description is the minimum required definition
scripts, references, and assets are optional supporting folders
progressive disclosure loads identity first, full guidance after matching, and heavy resources only during execution
the format is intended to remain usable across multiple agent tools rather than one vendor
project_value: Confirms the value of the project's existing narrow skill folders and small context paths.
Useful rule for keeping hundreds of reusable references without loading all of them into every task.
The new owner-learning assets should be routed through matching skills instead of injected wholesale.
items.guardrail: Visible adoption numbers were not independently verified.

items.visible_claim: Claude Code investment research using multiple methods and reported historical account results.
project_value: Do not import investment advice or performance claims.
Extract only the method: parallel independent analysis, conflicting conclusions, and explicit evidence comparison.
A screenshot of returns is not evidence that the method caused the returns.

visible_methods: four distinct analyst perspectives run in parallel and are allowed to disagree
numeric calculations use deterministic decimal handling and external verification
evidence is graded by confidence or source quality
red-line conditions reject a candidate regardless of an attractive score
reverse-thesis analysis asks what would make the current conclusion fail
research is decomposed into narrow reusable skills
project_value: Apply this structure to high-risk topic selection, source verification, product matching, and major architecture decisions.
Keep optimistic, skeptical, evidence, and operational perspectives separate until integration review.
Do not apply a rigid financial rubric to ordinary news or entertainment content.
items.guardrail: Financial use is outside current project scope; only the research discipline is retained.

visible_claims: cross-service call relationships modeled through a shared graph store
HTTP, gRPC, GraphQL, tRPC, and Socket.IO event relationships extracted across languages
Docker and Kubernetes artifacts represented in the same dependency graph
automatic initial indexing and background re-indexing on Git changes
self-update command and reduced configuration burden
project_value: Potential reference for tracing WorkflowEngine, modules, services, and data contracts.
The project already exposes Graphify capability, so overlap and added maintenance must be assessed before another graph indexer is considered.

visible_methods: compress ordinary explanatory prose while preserving code, commands, and errors byte-for-byte
use task-specific compression levels from light filler removal to extreme shorthand
project_value: Potentially useful for long tool outputs, handoff summaries, and repetitive agent explanation during weekly token constraints.
Compression level should depend on whether the text is brainstorming, review, execution instructions, or an owner-facing report.
items.guardrail: Reported quality and token reductions were not independently verified; compression must not remove uncertainty, approvals, evidence, or owner corrections.

visible_features: real-time savings and estimated cost display
compression of long memory or instruction files
automatic detection across several coding agents
project_value: Most useful as an optional transformation of duplicated prose, never as the canonical copy of project rules.
Canonical owner rules, safety boundaries, file paths, commands, and exact status must remain lossless.

visible_claims: fast cold start and small per-instance footprint
high sandbox density on one node
drop-in replacement path for an existing E2B-style SDK
items.project_value: Possible future reference for running untrusted or experimental agent code away from the owner workstation.
items.guardrail: Isolation and performance claims require security review and local feasibility testing before use.

visible_claims: search, page collection, crawling, and structured extraction in one workflow
competitor monitoring, lead collection, and research automation
reported official-document web coverage and latency metrics
project_value: Potential source adapter for bounded research collection when existing project collectors cannot access a needed site.
Useful output contract idea: source URL, extracted text, structured fields, crawl timestamp, and failure reason.
items.guardrail: Do not replace stable source-specific parsers or start broad crawling based only on the screenshot.

visible_claims: interacts with difficult webpages as part of collection
extracts web documents and includes PDF and DOCX parsing
project_value: Possible fallback for source pages, PDFs, or documents that current collectors cannot parse.
Any integration should preserve source attribution, cache behavior, retry limits, and diagnostic reasons.

tools.name: OpenConnector
tools.visible_claim: one gateway for many SaaS accounts through SDK, CLI, MCP, HTTP, or OpenAPI
tools.project_value: reference for centralized connection, scope, and audit-log management rather than giving every agent raw credentials

tools.name: Graphify
tools.visible_claim: turn a folder into a knowledge graph usable by a coding agent
tools.project_value: directly relevant because Graphify capability is already available in the project environment, though it was not exercised in this batch

items.guardrail: External SaaS writes, credentials, scopes, and connector installation require explicit review and approval.

cross_batch_learning: Large learning libraries require progressive disclosure so only matching references enter task context.
Parallel analysis is valuable when perspectives are independent and disagreement survives until integration.
Numeric claims require deterministic calculation, evidence grading, and failure-oriented checks.
Token compression may remove redundancy but must never compress away canonical rules or approval boundaries.
New graph, crawler, sandbox, and connector tools should be evaluated against capabilities already present before installation.
External app access should be centralized through scopes and logs rather than raw credentials distributed to agents.
